commands.review_summary:
The PR aggregates active per-session usage transcripts with same-stem reset/deleted archives across cache-backed summaries, direct summaries, time series, and logs.

PR surface: Source +165, Tests +171. Total +336 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current-main source and the linked issue comments show that active `<id>.jsonl` plus same-stem reset/deleted archives reproduce an undercount because per-session detail scans one file; I did not execute a live repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This open draft PR needs contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer review rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: fix(usage): include reset/deleted archives in per-session cost detail [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Per-session usage/cost views (`/usage` detail, dashboard per-session drilldown, time-series, logs, and the per-session cache) undercounted a logical session when an active `<id>.jsonl` existed alongside same-stem archives produced by `/new` and `/reset` (`<id>.jsonl.reset.<ts>`) or deletion (`<id>.jsonl.deleted.<ts>`).
- Global totals (`loadCostUsageSummary`) already count these archives via `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName`, but the per-session paths each scanned only one transcript, so a heavy `/new` rotation day shows a small fraction of real spend (issue example: ~$7 displayed vs ~$57 actual).
- This resolves the canonical session lineage once and aggregates the active transcript with its same-stem reset/deleted archives across `loadSessionCostSummary`, `loadSessionUsageTimeSeries`, `loadSessionLogs`, and `loadSessionCostSummaryFromCache`. The active `<id>.jsonl` stays the canonical `sessionFile`.
- Discovery uses the existing canonical usage-counted filename contract (`isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName`, `parseUsageCountedSessionIdFromFileName`, `isSessionArchiveArtifactName`, `parseSessionArchiveTimestamp`) rather than broad `startsWith` prefix matching, so `.bak` compaction siblings and checkpoint siblings stay excluded and cannot double-count.
- Out of scope: the global aggregation path (already archive-aware on `main`), the agent-facing `sessions_history`/`chat.history` family readers (separate surface, see #90239), and any config/schema changes.

> **AI-assisted PR.** Implemented and reviewed with AI assistance; the author has read and understands the diff and owns the final change.

## Linked context

Closes #46252

Related #46605 (closed — only the global `loadCostUsageSummary` aggregation landed on `main`; per-session detail was never addressed), #89289 (closed — earlier per-session attempt that ClawSweeper flagged for using broad prefix matching instead of the canonical usage-counted filename contract; this PR uses that contract).

Not requested by a maintainer; issue is labeled `clawsweeper:queueable-fix` / `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Per-session cost/usage detail, time-series, logs, and cache undercount a session whose `<id>.jsonl` has same-stem `.reset.`/`.deleted.` archives.
- Real environment tested: Local source checkout (macOS, Node 22.19, vitest infra config). New regression tests construct a real session directory layout — an active `<id>.jsonl` plus `<id>.jsonl.reset.<ts>` and `<id>.jsonl.deleted.<ts>` archives, and non-usage same-stem siblings (`.bak`/checkpoint) — and assert aggregated totals through the production load functions and the on-disk cache path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts`
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
  ```
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  54 passed (54)
  ```
  oxlint: exit 0 (no findings on the changed file).
- Observed result after fix: Per-session detail now sums the active transcript with its reset/deleted archives; non-usage siblings are excluded; the active `<id>.jsonl` remains the reported `sessionFile`. The three added tests fail on `main` (single-transcript scan) and pass with this change.
- What was not tested: A live end-to-end dashboard render against a populated `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions` directory with provider-billing cross-check (the issue's ~$7-vs-~$57 scenario) was not reproduced in a running gateway/UI. Behavior is covered at the load/cache layer that the dashboard consumes.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Draft PR — unit/integration-level proof only so far; live dashboard + provider-billing cross-check is the remaining verification step before marking ready.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` (54 passed), `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts` (clean).
- Regression coverage added (3 tests in `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts`):
  - combines cached active and archived same-stem session summaries (cache path),
  - combines active and archived same-stem transcripts for per-session detail queries (load path + time-series),
  - excludes non-usage same-stem siblings from per-session lineage totals (canonical-contract guard).
- What failed before this fix: the same three scenarios — per-session paths scanned only the active `<id>.jsonl` and dropped reset/deleted archive usage.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — per-session cost/usage figures increase to include reset/deleted archive transcripts (correcting an undercount). The reported `sessionFile` is unchanged.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No — no config, schema, default, or storage-shape changes; discovery reads the existing sessions directory only.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No — change is confined to backend usage aggregation and tests.
- Highest-risk area: lineage discovery picking up unintended same-stem siblings (double-count).
- How that risk is mitigated: discovery is gated by the canonical usage-counted filename contract (not prefix matching), with an explicit regression test asserting `.bak`/checkpoint siblings are excluded; archives are ordered deterministically by embedded timestamp; cumulative time-series totals are recomputed in chronological order after merging all lineage files.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer/owner review of the per-session aggregation approach; author to add live dashboard proof before marking ready.
- Waiting on: live-environment dashboard repro (author) and CI.
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: incorporates the ClawSweeper guidance from #89289 to use the canonical usage-counted filename contract for same-stem discovery instead of broad prefix matching.
target.author: harjothkhara
